ConstructorArguments: Time at which the timer reset happened, based on Time.Now.

Documentation.Summary: Time at which the timer reset happened, based on <see cref="P:Sandbox.Time.Now" />.

ConstructorArguments: Time passed since last reset, in seconds.

Documentation.Summary: Time passed since last reset, in seconds.

A convenience struct to easily measure time since an event last happened, based on .

Typical usage would see you assigning 0 to a variable of this type to reset the timer. Then the struct would return time since the last reset. i.e.: TimeSince lastUsed = 0; if ( lastUsed > 10 ) { /*Do something*/ }

ConstructorArguments: Time to which we are counting down to, based on Time.Now.

Documentation.Summary: Time to which we are counting down to, based on <see cref="P:Sandbox.Time.Now" />.
The actual countdown, in seconds.

ConstructorArguments: Amount of seconds passed since the countdown started.

Documentation.Summary: Amount of seconds passed since the countdown started.

ConstructorArguments: The countdown, but as a fraction, i.e. a value from 0 (start of countdown) to 1 (end of countdown)

Documentation.Summary: The countdown, but as a fraction, i.e. a value from 0 (start of countdown) to 1 (end of countdown)

A convenience struct to easily manage a time countdown, based on .

Typical usage would see you assigning to a variable of this type a necessary amount of seconds. Then the struct would return the time countdown, or can be used as a bool i.e.: TimeUntil nextAttack = 10; if ( nextAttack ) { /*Do something*/ }

ConstructorArguments: The type of the value currently stored in the Variant. This is automatically set when you assign a value to the Variant.

Documentation.Summary: The type of the value currently stored in the Variant. This is automatically set when you assign a value to the Variant.

ConstructorArguments: Gets or sets the value associated with this instance.

Documentation.Summary: Gets or sets the value associated with this instance.

A Variant is a type that can hold any value, and also keeps track of the type of the value it holds.
It's useful for cases where you want to store a value of an unknown type, or when you want to 
serialize/deserialize values of various types in a generic way.

ConstructorArguments: Cleans up resources used by the WebSocket client. This will also immediately close the connection if it is currently open.

Documentation.Summary: Cleans up resources used by the WebSocket client. This will also immediately close the connection if it is currently open.

ConstructorArguments: Add a sub-protocol to be negotiated during the WebSocket connection handshake.

Documentation.Summary: Add a sub-protocol to be negotiated during the WebSocket connection handshake.

ConstructorArguments: Establishes a connection to an external WebSocket service.

Documentation.Summary: Establishes a connection to an external WebSocket service.
Documentation.Return: A <see cref="T:System.Threading.Tasks.Task" /> which completes when the connection is established, or throws if it failed to connect.

Params.websocketUri: The WebSocket URI to connect to. For example, "ws://hostname.local:1280/" for unencrypted WebSocket or "wss://hostname.local:1281/" for encrypted.
Params.ct: A <see cref="T:System.Threading.CancellationToken" /> which allows the connection attempt to be aborted if necessary.

ConstructorArguments: Establishes a connection to an external WebSocket service.
Sends a text message to the WebSocket server.

Documentation.Summary: Sends a text message to the WebSocket server.
Documentation.Return: A <see cref="T:System.Threading.Tasks.ValueTask" /> which completes when the message was queued to be sent.

Params.message: The message text to send. Must not be null.

ConstructorArguments: Sends a binary message to the WebSocket server.

Documentation.Summary: Sends a binary message to the WebSocket server.
Documentation.Remarks: The <see cref="M:Sandbox.WebSocket.Send(System.ArraySegment{System.Byte})" /> and <see cref="M:Sandbox.WebSocket.Send(System.Span{System.Byte})" /> overloads allow sending subsections of byte arrays.
Documentation.Return: A <see cref="T:System.Threading.Tasks.ValueTask" /> which completes when the message was queued to be sent.

Params.data: The message data to send. Must not be null.

ConstructorArguments: Sends a binary message to the WebSocket server.
Sends a binary message to the WebSocket server.

Documentation.Summary: Initialized a new WebSocket client.

Params.maxMessageSize: The maximum message size to allow from the server, in bytes. Default 64 KiB.

ConstructorArguments: Get the sub-protocol that was negotiated during the opening handshake.

ConstructorArguments: Enable or disable compression for the websocket. If the server supports it, compression will be enabled for all messages. Note: compression is disabled by default, and can be dangerous if you are sending secrets across the network.

Documentation.Summary:
Enable or disable compression for the websocket. If the server supports it, compression will be enabled for all messages.
Note: compression is disabled by default, and can be dangerous if you are sending secrets across the network.

ConstructorArguments: A WebSocket client for connecting to external services.

Documentation.Summary: A WebSocket client for connecting to external services.
